glass-bubble-tangible.name: Glass Bubble Tangible
glass-bubble-tangible.description: A digital educational activity featuring an animated AI tutor presenting a concept about animation timing, showing two labeled bubble examples ("Fast Ease" and "Slow Linear") alongside three unlabeled bubble states for students to classify or match. The UI includes Repeat/Next/Check navigation buttons and a speech prompt instructing students to answer a question about the demonstrated animation states.

glass-bubble-breakdown.name: Glass Bubble Breakdown
glass-bubble-breakdown.description: A design breakdown slide explaining the layered construction of a glass bubble UI element, composed of four components: a mask layer, glass particles, a bubble base, and flare/reflections overlays. The visual demonstrates how iridescent, physics-responsive bubbles are built in Figma for what appears to be an interactive, visually engaging math toy interface.

10x10-groups.name: 10x10 Groups
10x10-groups.description: A guided multiplication/grouping activity displays 10 bags of oranges arranged in 2 rows of 5, asking students "How many groups are there?" with multiple-choice answers (10, 20, 100) and a "Check" button to validate their response.

12x5-groups.name: 12x5 Groups
12x5-groups.description: A multiple-choice math activity asking students to count **12 groups** (bags of oranges arranged in 2 rows of 6), with answer options 5, 12, and 60, guided by an animated tutor character and a "Check" button to confirm the selection.

examples-of-different-bag-sizes.name: Examples of Different Bag Sizes
examples-of-different-bag-sizes.description: A math grouping toy showing two rows of bags: the top row demonstrates groups of 1–8 items in progressively larger bags, while the bottom row illustrates varying bag capacities (2, 6, 10, 12, and 12 max items), with bag height/size visually representing capacity. The labeled "Max Items per Bag" row highlights how the same maximum (12) can appear in differently shaped containers, reinforcing the concept that quantity is independent of container shape.

stacks.name: Stacks
stacks.description: A stacks visualization tool showing three containers with 2, 6, and 10 colored horizontal bar items respectively, demonstrating increasing stack heights for teaching grouping/counting concepts. Each stack has a distinct outer container border separate from its colored inner items, with a teacher note emphasizing the importance of the outer container for highlighting during instruction and remediation.

12x12-groups.name: 12x12 Groups
12x12-groups.description: A multiple-choice math activity asking students to count 12 groups of oranges (arranged in a 4×3 grid of trays), with answer options 5, 12, and 60, featuring an AI tutor character, and Repeat/Next/Check navigation buttons.

20x2-groups.name: 20x2 Groups
20x2-groups.description: A multiple-choice math activity asks Grade 3 students "How many groups are there?" displaying a 4×5 grid of 20 fruit bags (each containing 2 items), with answer options 2, 20, and 40. An AI tutor character provides scaffolding via a dialogue bubble, and a "Check" button confirms the answer selection.

20x2-semi-abstract-groups.name: 20x2 Semi-Abstract Groups
20x2-semi-abstract-groups.description: A multiple-choice math activity asking students to count **16 groups** of 2 dots each (arranged in a 4-column grid), with answer options of 2, 5, or 10 — none of which are correct — alongside a virtual tutor avatar, Repeat audio button, and a disabled Check button awaiting selection.

12x5-semi-abstract-groups.name: 12x5 Semi-Abstract Groups
12x5-semi-abstract-groups.description: A multiple-choice math activity asks students "How many groups are there?" displaying 10 oval groups (arranged in 2 rows of 5) each containing 6 dots, with answer options 2, 5, and 10, and a "Check" button to confirm the selection.

10x10-semi-abstract-groups.name: 10x10 Semi-Abstract Groups
10x10-semi-abstract-groups.description: A multiple-choice math activity asks students to count **10 groups** of dot-filled capsules (arranged in a 5×2 grid), with answer options 2, 5, or 10 displayed as radio buttons below. The semi-abstract scaffold shows grouped dot arrays rather than individual countable objects, with a "Check" button to confirm the answer and a virtual tutor character providing audio instruction via a "Repeat" button.

12x12-semi-abstract-groups.name: 12x12 Semi-Abstract Groups
12x12-semi-abstract-groups.description: A multiple-choice math activity displays 12 groups of 12 dots (arranged in pill-shaped containers, 6 per row across 2 rows) asking students to identify the number of groups. The UI shows three answer options (2, 5, 10) with radio buttons and a yellow "Check" button, representing a semi-abstract scaffold level where concrete dot arrays support the counting concept.

12x12-semi-abstract-groups-horizontal.name: 12x12 Semi-Abstract Groups Horizontal
12x12-semi-abstract-groups-horizontal.description: A multiple-choice math activity asks students to identify the number of groups shown, displaying **12 horizontal oval groups** each containing **10 circles** (semi-abstract representation), with answer options **2, 5, and 10** at the bottom and a **Check button** to validate the response.

multi-select.name: Multi Select
multi-select.description: A multi-select math activity where students identify which picture represents 2×6 (2 groups of 6), with four answer choices showing different groupings of pink oval objects; the bottom-left option (showing 2 groups of 6) is checked as selected, the top-right option (showing 12 items in one row) is being hovered, and a "Check" button is available to submit.

counting-group-total-on-screen.name: Counting Group Total On Screen
counting-group-total-on-screen.description: A digital counting activity presents 3 bags of 4 oranges each, asking "How many groups are there?" with a partially scaffolded state showing purple "4" labels under the first two bags (but not the third), and three multiple-choice answer options (3, 4, 5) at the bottom with a "Check" button.

interactive-construction-toy.name: Interactive Construction Toy
interactive-construction-toy.description: An interactive division/multiplication problem where students must determine how many groups of 4 equal 12, with a fill-in-the-blank equation (12 = ☐ × 4) and a number tile selector (1–5) for input. The scaffold presents the problem in text and symbolic form simultaneously, with a 3D avatar, Repeat audio button, and a Check button for answer validation.

glows-on-groups.name: Glows on Groups
glows-on-groups.description: A multiple-choice math grouping activity where students select the correct visual representation of a multiplication/equal-groups concept, showing 4 rows of answer options (blob pairs, money bags, and rectangles) with the **middle column items highlighted/glowing** to indicate current selection. The UI includes a virtual tutor avatar, a "Repeat" audio button, and a "Check" button to confirm the answer.

module-7-warm-up.name: Module 7 Warm-Up
module-7-warm-up.description: A pictograph reading activity where students interpret a shop's cookie sales data, showing 4 pecan cookie symbols on a picture graph with a key indicating each symbol equals 5 cookies (total = 20). The UI includes a virtual avatar instructor, a "Repeat" audio button, and "Next"/"Check" navigation buttons, suggesting a guided warm-up with scaffolded instruction before student input.

group-builder.name: Group Builder
group-builder.description: An interactive grouping activity where Grade 3 students use **+/−** stepper controls to set the number of **Bags** and **Oranges** to solve the prompt "Make 3 bags of 5 oranges," with both counters currently at zero/default state. The UI features a 3D avatar instructor, a "Check" confirmation button, and a "Repeat" audio button for accessibility support.

groups-to-pictograph-v3.name: Groups to Pictograph v3
groups-to-pictograph-v3.description: A Grade 3 educational math game screen showing a completed pictograph titled "Minis" displaying collected data across three color categories (Red: 8, Blue: 6, Yellow: 4), with each circle representing 1 Mini. The UI includes a virtual avatar tutor, a "Next" button to proceed, and a "Repeat" button to replay the audio instruction "Here is the data you collected!"

making-groups-v3.name: Making Groups V3
making-groups-v3.description: An interactive math activity prompting students to create 3 groups of 5 oranges using a stepper control (currently at 0 bags), featuring a 3D animated tutor character, a "Check" button, and a "Repeat" audio option for instruction playback.
